column4: If ye walk in {{c1::My}} statutes, and keep My commandments, and do them;
If ye walk in My {{c1::statutes}}, and keep My commandments, and do them;
then {{c1::I}} will give your rains in their season, and the land shall yield her produce, and the trees of the field shall yield their fruit.
then I will give your rains in their {{c1::season}}, and the land shall yield her produce, and the trees of the field shall yield their fruit.
And your {{c1::threshing}} shall reach unto the vintage, and the vintage shall reach unto the sowing time; and ye shall eat your bread until ye have enough, and dwell in your land safely.
And your threshing shall reach unto the {{c1::vintage}}, and the vintage shall reach unto the sowing time; and ye shall eat your bread until ye have enough, and dwell in your land safely.
And {{c1::I}} will give peace in the land, and ye shall lie down, and none shall make you afraid; and I will cause evil beasts to cease out of the land, neither shall the sword go through your land.
And I will give peace in the land, and ye shall lie down, and none shall make you {{c1::afraid}}; and I will cause evil beasts to cease out of the land, neither shall the sword go through your land.
And ye shall chase your {{c1::enemies}}, and they shall fall before you by the sword.
And ye shall chase your enemies, and they shall fall {{c1::before}} you by the sword.
And five of you shall chase a {{c1::hundred}}, and a hundred of you shall chase ten thousand; and your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.
And five of you shall chase a hundred, and a hundred of you shall chase ten {{c1::thousand}}; and your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.
And {{c1::I}} will have respect unto you, and make you fruitful, and multiply you; and will establish My covenant with you.
And I will have respect unto you, and make you fruitful, and multiply you; and will establish {{c1::My}} covenant with you.
And ye shall eat old store long kept, and ye shall bring forth the old from {{c1::before}} the new.
And {{c1::I}} will set My tabernacle among you, and My soul shall not abhor you.
And I will set {{c1::My}} tabernacle among you, and My soul shall not abhor you.
And {{c1::I}} will walk among you, and will be your God, and ye shall be My people.
And I will walk among you, and will be your God, and ye shall be {{c1::My}} people.
I am the LORD your God, who brought you forth out of the land of {{c1::Egypt}}, that ye should not be their bondmen; and I have broken the bars of your yoke, and made you go upright.
I am the LORD your God, who {{c1::brought}} you forth out of the land of Egypt, that ye should not be their bondmen; and I have broken the bars of your yoke, and made you go upright.
But if ye will not hearken unto {{c1::Me}}, and will not do all these commandments;
But if ye will not {{c1::hearken}} unto Me, and will not do all these commandments;
and if ye shall reject {{c1::My}} statutes, and if your soul abhor Mine ordinances, so that ye will not do all My commandments, but break My covenant;
and if ye shall reject My statutes, and if your soul abhor {{c1::Mine}} ordinances, so that ye will not do all My commandments, but break My covenant;

I also will do this unto you: I will {{c1::appoint}} terror over you, even consumption and fever, that shall make the eyes to fail, and the soul to languish; and ye shall sow your seed in vain, for your enemies shall eat it.

I also will do this unto you: I will appoint {{c1::terror}} over you, even consumption and fever, that shall make the eyes to fail, and the soul to languish; and ye shall sow your seed in vain, for your enemies shall eat it.
And {{c1::I}} will set My face against you, and ye shall be smitten before your enemies; they that hate you shall rule over you; and ye shall flee when none pursueth you.
And I will set {{c1::My}} face against you, and ye shall be smitten before your enemies; they that hate you shall rule over you; and ye shall flee when none pursueth you.
And if ye will not yet for these things hearken unto {{c1::Me}}, then I will chastise you seven times more for your sins.
And if ye will not yet for these things hearken unto Me, then {{c1::I}} will chastise you seven times more for your sins.
And {{c1::I}} will break the pride of your power; and I will make your heaven as iron, and your earth as brass.
And I will break the pride of your power; and I will make your {{c1::heaven}} as iron, and your earth as brass.
And your {{c1::strength}} shall be spent in vain; for your land shall not yield her produce, neither shall the trees of the land yield their fruit.
And your strength shall be spent in vain; for your land shall not yield her {{c1::produce}}, neither shall the trees of the land yield their fruit.
And if ye walk contrary unto {{c1::Me}}, and will not hearken unto Me; I will bring seven times more plagues upon you according to your sins.
And if ye walk contrary unto Me, and will not hearken unto Me; {{c1::I}} will bring seven times more plagues upon you according to your sins.
And {{c1::I}} will send the beast of the field among you, which shall rob you of your children, and destroy your cattle, and make you few in number; and your ways shall become desolate.
And I will send the beast of the field among you, which shall rob you of your {{c1::children}}, and destroy your cattle, and make you few in number; and your ways shall become desolate.
And if in spite of these things ye will not be corrected unto {{c1::Me}}, but will walk contrary unto Me;
And if in spite of these {{c1::things}} ye will not be corrected unto Me, but will walk contrary unto Me;
then will {{c1::I}} also walk contrary unto you; and I will smite you, even I, seven times for your sins.
then will I also walk {{c1::contrary}} unto you; and I will smite you, even I, seven times for your sins.

And {{c1::I}} will bring a sword upon you, that shall execute the vengeance of the covenant; and ye shall be gathered together within your cities; and I will send the pestilence among you; and ye shall be delivered into the hand of the enemy.

And I will bring a sword upon you, that shall {{c1::execute}} the vengeance of the covenant; and ye shall be gathered together within your cities; and I will send the pestilence among you; and ye shall be delivered into the hand of the enemy.
When {{c1::I}} break your staff of bread, ten women shall bake your bread in one oven, and they shall deliver your bread again by weight; and ye shall eat, and not be satisfied.
When I break your staff of bread, ten women shall bake your bread in one oven, and they shall {{c1::deliver}} your bread again by weight; and ye shall eat, and not be satisfied.
And if ye will not for all this hearken unto {{c1::Me}}, but walk contrary unto Me;
And if ye will not for all this {{c1::hearken}} unto Me, but walk contrary unto Me;
then {{c1::I}} will walk contrary unto you in fury; and I also will chastise you seven times for your sins.
then I will walk {{c1::contrary}} unto you in fury; and I also will chastise you seven times for your sins.
And ye shall eat the flesh of your sons, and the flesh of your {{c1::daughters}} shall ye eat.
And {{c1::I}} will destroy your high places, and cut down your sun-pillars, and cast your carcasses upon the carcasses of your idols; and My soul shall abhor you.
And I will destroy your high places, and cut down your sun-pillars, and cast your carcasses upon the carcasses of your idols; and {{c1::My}} soul shall abhor you.
And {{c1::I}} will make your cities a waste, and will bring your sanctuaries unto desolation, and I will not smell the savour of your sweet odours.
And I will make your {{c1::cities}} a waste, and will bring your sanctuaries unto desolation, and I will not smell the savour of your sweet odours.
And {{c1::I}} will bring the land into desolation; and your enemies that dwell therein shall be astonished at it.
And I will bring the land into {{c1::desolation}}; and your enemies that dwell therein shall be astonished at it.
And you will {{c1::I}} scatter among the nations, and I will draw out the sword after you; and your land shall be a desolation, and your cities shall be a waste.
And you will I {{c1::scatter}} among the nations, and I will draw out the sword after you; and your land shall be a desolation, and your cities shall be a waste.
Then shall the land be paid her {{c1::sabbaths}}, as long as it lieth desolate, and ye are in your enemies’land; even then shall the land rest, and repay her sabbaths.
Then shall the land be paid her sabbaths, as long as it lieth {{c1::desolate}}, and ye are in your enemies’land; even then shall the land rest, and repay her sabbaths.
As long as it lieth {{c1::desolate}} it shall have rest; even the rest which it had not in your sabbaths, when ye dwelt upon it.
As long as it lieth desolate it shall have rest; even the rest which it had not in your {{c1::sabbaths}}, when ye dwelt upon it.

And as for them that are left of you, {{c1::I}} will send a faintness into their heart in the lands of their enemies; and the sound of a driven leaf shall chase them; and they shall flee, as one fleeth from the sword; and they shall fall when none pursueth.

And as for them that are left of you, I will send a {{c1::faintness}} into their heart in the lands of their enemies; and the sound of a driven leaf shall chase them; and they shall flee, as one fleeth from the sword; and they shall fall when none pursueth.
And they shall {{c1::stumble}} one upon another, as it were before the sword, when none pursueth; and ye shall have no power to stand before your enemies.
And they shall stumble one upon {{c1::another}}, as it were before the sword, when none pursueth; and ye shall have no power to stand before your enemies.
And ye shall {{c1::perish}} among the nations, and the land of your enemies shall eat you up.
And ye shall perish among the {{c1::nations}}, and the land of your enemies shall eat you up.
And they that are left of you shall pine away in their {{c1::iniquity}} in your enemies’lands; and also in the iniquities of their fathers shall they pine away with them.
And they that are left of you shall pine away in their iniquity in your {{c1::enemies’lands}}; and also in the iniquities of their fathers shall they pine away with them.
And they shall confess their iniquity, and the iniquity of their fathers, in their treachery which they committed against {{c1::Me}}, and also that they have walked contrary unto Me.
And they shall {{c1::confess}} their iniquity, and the iniquity of their fathers, in their treachery which they committed against Me, and also that they have walked contrary unto Me.

I also will walk {{c1::contrary}} unto them, and bring them into the land of their enemies; if then perchance their uncircumcised heart be humbled, and they then be paid the punishment of their iniquity;

I also will walk contrary unto them, and bring them into the land of their {{c1::enemies}}; if then perchance their uncircumcised heart be humbled, and they then be paid the punishment of their iniquity;
then will {{c1::I}} remember My covenant with Jacob, and also My covenant with Isaac, and also My covenant with Abraham will I remember; and I will remember the land.
then will I remember {{c1::My}} covenant with Jacob, and also My covenant with Isaac, and also My covenant with Abraham will I remember; and I will remember the land.

For the land shall lie forsaken without them, and shall be paid her sabbaths, while she lieth desolate without them; and they shall be paid the punishment of their iniquity; because, even because they rejected {{c1::Mine}} ordinances, and their soul abhorred My statutes.

For the land shall lie forsaken without them, and shall be paid her sabbaths, while she lieth desolate without them; and they shall be paid the punishment of their iniquity; because, even because they rejected Mine ordinances, and their soul abhorred {{c1::My}} statutes.

And yet for all that, when they are in the land of their enemies, {{c1::I}} will not reject them, neither will I abhor them, to destroy them utterly, and to break My covenant with them; for I am the LORD their God.

And yet for all that, when they are in the land of their enemies, I will not reject them, neither will I abhor them, to destroy them utterly, and to break {{c1::My}} covenant with them; for I am the LORD their God.
But {{c1::I}} will for their sakes remember the covenant of their ancestors, whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt in the sight of the nations, that I might be their God: I am the LORD.
But I will for their sakes remember the covenant of their ancestors, whom I brought forth out of the land of {{c1::Egypt}} in the sight of the nations, that I might be their God: I am the LORD.
These are the statutes and ordinances and laws, which the LORD made between Him and the children of {{c1::Israel}} in mount Sinai by the hand of Moses.
These are the statutes and ordinances and laws, which the LORD made between Him and the children of Israel in mount {{c1::Sinai}} by the hand of Moses.
And the LORD spoke unto {{c1::Moses}}, saying:
And the LORD spoke unto Moses, {{c1::saying}}:
Speak unto the children of {{c1::Israel}}, and say unto them: When a man shall clearly utter a vow of persons unto the LORD, according to thy valuation,
Speak unto the {{c1::children}} of Israel, and say unto them: When a man shall clearly utter a vow of persons unto the LORD, according to thy valuation,
then thy {{c1::valuation}} shall be for the male from twenty years old even unto sixty years old, even thy valuation shall be fifty shekels of silver, after the shekel of the sanctuary.
then thy valuation shall be for the male from {{c1::twenty}} years old even unto sixty years old, even thy valuation shall be fifty shekels of silver, after the shekel of the sanctuary.
And if it be a {{c1::female}}, then thy valuation shall be thirty shekels.
And if it be a female, then thy {{c1::valuation}} shall be thirty shekels.
And if it be from five years old even unto {{c1::twenty}} years old, then thy valuation shall be for the male twenty shekels, and for the female ten shekels.
And if it be from five years old even unto twenty years old, then thy {{c1::valuation}} shall be for the male twenty shekels, and for the female ten shekels.
And if it be from a month old even unto five years old, then thy {{c1::valuation}} shall be for the male five shekels of silver, and for the female thy valuation shall be three shekels of silver.
And if it be from a month old even unto five years old, then thy valuation shall be for the male five {{c1::shekels}} of silver, and for the female thy valuation shall be three shekels of silver.
And if it be from sixty years old and {{c1::upward}}: if it be a male, then thy valuation shall be fifteen shekels, and for the female ten shekels.
And if it be from sixty years old and upward: if it be a male, then thy {{c1::valuation}} shall be fifteen shekels, and for the female ten shekels.
But if he be too poor for thy {{c1::valuation}}, then he shall be set before the priest, and the priest shall value him; according to the means of him that vowed shall the priest value him.
But if he be too poor for thy valuation, then he shall be set {{c1::before}} the priest, and the priest shall value him; according to the means of him that vowed shall the priest value him.
And if it be a beast, {{c1::whereof}} men bring an offering unto the LORD, all that any man giveth of such unto the LORD shall be holy.
And if it be a beast, whereof men bring an {{c1::offering}} unto the LORD, all that any man giveth of such unto the LORD shall be holy.
He shall not alter it, nor {{c1::change}} it, a good for a bad, or a bad for a good; and if he shall at all change beast for beast, then both it and that for which it is changed shall be holy.
He shall not alter it, nor change it, a good for a bad, or a bad for a good; and if he shall at all change beast for beast, then both it and that for which it is {{c1::changed}} shall be holy.
And if it be any {{c1::unclean}} beast, of which they may not bring an offering unto the LORD, then he shall set the beast before the priest.
And if it be any unclean beast, of which they may not bring an {{c1::offering}} unto the LORD, then he shall set the beast before the priest.
And the {{c1::priest}} shall value it, whether it be good or bad; as thou the priest valuest it, so shall it be.
And the priest shall value it, {{c1::whether}} it be good or bad; as thou the priest valuest it, so shall it be.
But if he will {{c1::indeed}} redeem it, then he shall add the fifth part thereof unto thy valuation.
But if he will indeed {{c1::redeem}} it, then he shall add the fifth part thereof unto thy valuation.
And when a man shall {{c1::sanctify}} his house to be holy unto the LORD, then the priest shall value it, whether it be good or bad; as the priest shall value it, so shall it stand.
And when a man shall sanctify his house to be holy unto the LORD, then the {{c1::priest}} shall value it, whether it be good or bad; as the priest shall value it, so shall it stand.
And if he that {{c1::sanctified}} it will redeem his house, then he shall add the fifth part of the money of thy valuation unto it, and it shall be his.
And if he that sanctified it will {{c1::redeem}} his house, then he shall add the fifth part of the money of thy valuation unto it, and it shall be his.

And if a man shall {{c1::sanctify}} unto the LORD part of the field of his possession, then thy valuation shall be according to the sowing thereof; the sowing of a homer of barley shall be valued at fifty shekels of silver.

And if a man shall sanctify unto the LORD part of the field of his {{c1::possession}}, then thy valuation shall be according to the sowing thereof; the sowing of a homer of barley shall be valued at fifty shekels of silver.
If he {{c1::sanctify}} his field from the year of jubilee, according to thy valuation it shall stand.
If he sanctify his field from the year of {{c1::jubilee}}, according to thy valuation it shall stand.

But if he {{c1::sanctify}} his field after the jubilee, then the priest shall reckon unto him the money according to the years that remain unto the year of jubilee, and an abatement shall be made from thy valuation.

But if he sanctify his field after the {{c1::jubilee}}, then the priest shall reckon unto him the money according to the years that remain unto the year of jubilee, and an abatement shall be made from thy valuation.
And if he that {{c1::sanctified}} the field will indeed redeem it, then he shall add the fifth part of the money of thy valuation unto it, and it shall be assured to him.
And if he that sanctified the field will {{c1::indeed}} redeem it, then he shall add the fifth part of the money of thy valuation unto it, and it shall be assured to him.
And if he will not {{c1::redeem}} the field, or if he have sold the field to another man, it shall not be redeemed any more.
And if he will not redeem the field, or if he have sold the field to {{c1::another}} man, it shall not be redeemed any more.
But the field, when it goeth out in the {{c1::jubilee}}, shall be holy unto the LORD, as a field devoted; the possession thereof shall be the priest’s.
But the field, when it goeth out in the jubilee, shall be holy unto the LORD, as a field {{c1::devoted}}; the possession thereof shall be the priest’s.
And if he {{c1::sanctify}} unto the LORD a field which he hath bought, which is not of the field of his possession;
And if he sanctify unto the LORD a field which he hath {{c1::bought}}, which is not of the field of his possession;
then the {{c1::priest}} shall reckon unto him the worth of thy valuation unto the year of jubilee; and he shall give thy valuation in that day, as a holy thing unto the LORD.
then the priest shall {{c1::reckon}} unto him the worth of thy valuation unto the year of jubilee; and he shall give thy valuation in that day, as a holy thing unto the LORD.
In the year of {{c1::jubilee}} the field shall return unto him of whom it was bought, even to him to whom the possession of the land belongeth.
In the year of jubilee the field shall {{c1::return}} unto him of whom it was bought, even to him to whom the possession of the land belongeth.
And all thy {{c1::valuations}} shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary; twenty gerahs shall be the shekel.
And all thy valuations shall be {{c1::according}} to the shekel of the sanctuary; twenty gerahs shall be the shekel.
Howbeit the firstling among beasts, which is born as a firstling to the LORD, no man shall sanctify it; whether it be ox or sheep, it is the {{c1::LORD’S}}.
{{c1::Howbeit}} the firstling among beasts, which is born as a firstling to the LORD, no man shall sanctify it; whether it be ox or sheep, it is the LORD’S.

And if it be of an {{c1::unclean}} beast, then he shall ransom it according to thy valuation, and shall add unto it the fifth part thereof; or if it be not redeemed, then it shall be sold according to thy valuation.

And if it be of an unclean beast, then he shall {{c1::ransom}} it according to thy valuation, and shall add unto it the fifth part thereof; or if it be not redeemed, then it shall be sold according to thy valuation.

{{c1::Notwithstanding}}, no devoted thing, that a man may devote unto the LORD of all that he hath, whether of man or beast, or of the field of his possession, shall be sold or redeemed; every devoted thing is most holy unto the LORD.

Notwithstanding, no {{c1::devoted}} thing, that a man may devote unto the LORD of all that he hath, whether of man or beast, or of the field of his possession, shall be sold or redeemed; every devoted thing is most holy unto the LORD.
None {{c1::devoted}}, that may be devoted of men, shall be ransomed; he shall surely be put to death.
None devoted, that may be devoted of men, shall be {{c1::ransomed}}; he shall surely be put to death.
And all the tithe of the land, whether of the seed of the land, or of the fruit of the tree, is the {{c1::LORD’S}}; it is holy unto the LORD.
And all the tithe of the land, {{c1::whether}} of the seed of the land, or of the fruit of the tree, is the LORD’S; it is holy unto the LORD.
And if a man will {{c1::redeem}} aught of his tithe, he shall add unto it the fifth part thereof.
And if a man will redeem aught of his tithe, he shall add unto it the fifth part {{c1::thereof}}.
And all the tithe of the herd or the flock, {{c1::whatsoever}} passeth under the rod, the tenth shall be holy unto the LORD.
And all the tithe of the herd or the flock, whatsoever {{c1::passeth}} under the rod, the tenth shall be holy unto the LORD.
He shall not {{c1::inquire}} whether it be good or bad, neither shall he change it; and if he change it at all, then both it and that for which it is changed shall be holy; it shall not be redeemed.
He shall not inquire {{c1::whether}} it be good or bad, neither shall he change it; and if he change it at all, then both it and that for which it is changed shall be holy; it shall not be redeemed.
These are the commandments, which the LORD commanded {{c1::Moses}} for the children of Israel in mount Sinai.
These are the commandments, which the LORD commanded Moses for the children of {{c1::Israel}} in mount Sinai.
